source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
On December 17, 2019, China's first domestically built aircraft carrier, the "Shandong," was delivered to the navy at a military port in Sanya, Hainan. President Xi Jinping, Chairman of the Central Military Commission, attended the commissioning ceremony. With the advancement of technology, future Chinese aircraft carriers will be equipped with electromagnetic catapults to shorten the takeoff distance of aircraft. As shown in the figure, the total length of the horizontal runway of an aircraft carrier is $l$, and a linear motor is installed in the electromagnetic catapult zone, which can provide a constant traction force of $4.0 \times 10^{4} \, \mathrm{N}$. An aircraft with a mass of $m = 2.0 \times 10^{4} \, \mathrm{kg}$ has a jet engine that can provide a constant thrust of $1.2 \times 10^{5} \, \mathrm{N}$. Assuming that the resistance force on the aircraft during the runway is always 0.2 times the weight of the aircraft, the speed at the end of the runway when taking off from the carrier is $\upsilon = 36 \, \mathrm{m/s}$. The time taken for the aircraft to move in the latter part of the runway is 4 seconds longer than the time taken in the catapult zone. Assuming the aircraft carrier remains stationary and the aircraft can be treated as a point mass with constant mass, the gravitational acceleration is $g = 10 \, \mathrm{m/s}^{2}$.

1. Find the speed of the aircraft as it leaves the electromagnetic catapult zone.
2. Find the length $x$ of the electromagnetic launch zone
3. Find the total length of the runway $l$

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows 1 available image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: The problem asks for three linked quantities: speed after catapult zone, catapult-zone length x, and total runway length l
Solving requires combining force/acceleration information, kinematics on two runway segments, and the stated time difference between segments
question_text is long and contains multiple physical conditions rather than a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and answer_text provides three explicit numeric results with units
The quantities are standard physics outputs that can be checked by equation-based derivation

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, and registry_passed=true with no recorded asset issues
initial scoring is strong: image_dependency_score=0.82, multi_step_score=0.9, verifiability_score=0.94
the sample appears recoverable and useful as a multi-step physics problem with explicit numeric answers
the only noted risk is partial uncertainty about how necessary the image is from text alone, but this does not indicate missing core semantic support

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
On December 17, 2019, China's first domestically built aircraft carrier, the "Shandong," was delivered to the navy at a military port in Sanya, Hainan. President Xi Jinping, Chairman of the Central Military Commission, attended the commissioning ceremony. With the advancement of technology, future Chinese aircraft carriers will be equipped with electromagnetic catapults to shorten the takeoff distance of aircraft. As shown in the figure, the total length of the horizontal runway of an aircraft carrier is $l$, and a linear motor is installed in the electromagnetic catapult zone, which can provide a constant traction force of $4.0 \times 10^{4}\,\mathrm{N}$. An aircraft with a mass of $m = 2.0 \times 10^{4}\,\mathrm{kg}$ has a jet engine that can provide a constant thrust of $1.2 \times 10^{5}\,\mathrm{N}$. Assuming that the resistance force on the aircraft during the runway is always 0.2 times the weight of the aircraft, the speed at the end of the runway when taking off from the carrier is $\upsilon = 36\,\mathrm{m/s}$. The time taken for the aircraft to move in the latter part of the runway is 4 seconds longer than the time taken in the catapult zone. Assuming the aircraft carrier remains stationary and the aircraft can be treated as a point mass with constant mass, the gravitational acceleration is $g = 10\,\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$.

1. Find the speed of the aircraft as it leaves the electromagnetic catapult zone.
2. Find the length $x$ of the electromagnetic launch zone.
3. Find the total length of the runway $l$.
normalization_notes: Question explicitly references a figure and force_requires_image=true.

President Xi Jinping, Chairman of the Central Military Commission, attended the commissioning ceremony. With the advancement of technology, future Chinese aircraft carriers will be equipped with electromagnetic catapults to shorten the takeoff distance of aircraft. As shown in the figure, the total length of the horizontal runway of an aircraft carrier is $l$, and a linear motor is installed in the electromagnetic catapult zone, which can provide a constant traction force of $4.0 \times 10^{4}\,\mathrm{N}$. An aircraft with a mass of $m = 2.0 \times 10^{4}\,\mathrm{kg}$ has a jet engine that can provide a constant thrust of $1.2 \times 10^{5}\,\mathrm{N}$. Assuming that the resistance force on the aircraft during the runway is always 0.2 times the weight of the aircraft, the speed at the end of the runway when taking off from the carrier is $\upsilon = 36\,\mathrm{m/s}$. The time taken for the aircraft to move in the latter part of the runway is 4 seconds longer than the time taken in the catapult zone. Assuming the aircraft carrier remains stationary and the aircraft can be treated as a point mass with constant mass, the gravitational acceleration is $g = 10\,\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$.

1. Find the speed of the aircraft as it leaves the electromagnetic catapult zone.
2. Find the length $x$ of the electromagnetic launch zone.
3. Find the total length of the runway $l$.

agent_assessment.rationale: The physics problem statement is complete and clearly asks for three quantities. The image is a simple schematic showing the electromagnetic catapult section of length x within the total runway length l; despite being visually simple, it is semantically usable and consistent with the text.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The problem is complete and understandable, the answer is fully provided, the rewrite is usable as-is, and the text-image alignment and solvability evidence show no serious issues.
